issues.description: The diff hunk header says `export function createSelectionManager(` but the actual function in the file is `export function createSelection(` (line 46). This means the diff is either out of date with the file or was generated against a different version. Applying it as-is is suspect — at minimum it indicates the change was not exercised against the real codebase.
issues.suggested_fix: Regenerate the diff against the current `createSelection` function and verify it applies cleanly before merging.

issues.description: `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` are added as inner functions but are NOT added to the return statement at line 262 (`return { setHovered, setSelected, refreshSelected, fitToView, flyToEntity, invalidateRect, dispose }`). They are also NOT added to the `SelectionContext` interface at lines 29-37. The functions are therefore unreachable from any consumer — the measurement tool cited in the doc comment cannot actually call them. This is dead code as written.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` to both the `SelectionContext` interface and the returned object: `return { ..., focusOnBounds, focusOnEntities, ..., dispose };`.

issues.description: `focusOnBounds` duplicates the camera-positioning logic that already exists in `fitToView` (lines 200-217) and `flyToEntity` (lines 229-245). All three blocks compute center/size, derive distance from FOV, position camera along view direction, call lookAt, update projection, and copy to controls.target. With this PR there are now THREE near-identical copies of the same routine. This is exactly the kind of duplication the new helper should be eliminating, not adding to.
issues.suggested_fix: Refactor `fitToView` and `flyToEntity` to delegate to `focusOnBounds`. For example: `function fitToView() { const box = new Box3(); for (const m of getMeshes().values()) box.expandByObject(m); focusOnBounds(box, 1.0); }` and `function flyToEntity(path) { focusOnEntities([path]); }`.

issues.description: The `padding: number = 1.2` parameter is misleadingly named. It is actually a scale multiplier (1.0 = exact fit, 1.2 = 20% extra room), not a padding distance. A reader who sees `focusOnBounds(box, 5)` would reasonably expect 5 units of padding, but it would actually scale the visible region by 5×.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to `paddingFactor`, `scale`, or `marginFactor` to make the multiplicative semantics explicit, and document the scale convention in the JSDoc.

issues.description: No tests are added for the new `focusOnBounds` or `focusOnEntities` functions, even though the existing test file has comprehensive coverage of `flyToEntity` (e.g. lines 731-799 cover the no-op-on-unknown case, view-direction offset, single-mesh expansion). The new functions add new failure modes (empty entityPaths array, all-unknown paths, multi-mesh expansion combining bounds, padding parameter) that should be tested at the same level.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests covering: (a) `focusOnEntities([])` is a no-op; (b) `focusOnEntities(['Unknown'])` is a no-op and does not throw; (c) `focusOnEntities(['A','B'])` calls `expandByObject` for both meshes; (d) `focusOnBounds` with empty Box3 is a no-op; (e) padding parameter affects camera distance proportionally.

issues.description: The doc comment claims this is 'Used by the measurement tool' but a search of the gui/ tree finds no measurement tool referencing `focusOnBounds` or `focusOnEntities`. Per project convention (`feedback_no_throwaway.md`), prefer minimal real implementations that wire up an actual consumer rather than speculative APIs. Adding unused public surface area also makes future refactoring harder.
issues.suggested_fix: Either land this together with the measurement tool that consumes it, or hold the change until the consumer is ready. If shipping now, add a TODO referencing the task that will wire it up.

issues.description: `viewDir.multiplyScalar(distance)` mutates `viewDir` in place, which is fine here since the local variable is unused afterward, but it follows a pattern that quietly bites readers. Note also that the function does not check `isDisposed` before mutating `camera`/`controls`, so a call after `dispose()` will still rotate the camera. This matches the existing `flyToEntity` behavior, so it is not a regression, but is worth a guard.
issues.suggested_fix: Optionally add `if (isDisposed) return;` at the top of `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` for defensive consistency. Consider using `viewDir.clone().multiplyScalar(distance)` for clarity, or extract a `positionCameraToFrame(center, maxDim)` helper used by all three call sites.

issues.description: `focusOnEntities` calls `getMeshes()` once per iteration in the loop body. `getMeshes` is a callback that may rebuild the map; for a large `entityPaths` list this is O(n) callback invocations. Minor for typical UI usage but a free optimization.
issues.suggested_fix: Hoist the call: `const meshes = getMeshes(); for (const path of entityPaths) { const mesh = meshes.get(path); ... }`.

issues.description: `camera.fov` is assumed to be defined and nonzero. If `camera` is ever an `OrthographicCamera` (or fov is somehow 0), `Math.tan(0) === 0` causes division by zero, producing `Infinity` for `distance` and corrupting camera position. This matches the existing `fitToView`/`flyToEntity` pattern, so not a new bug, but the new helper would be a good place to centralize the guard.
issues.suggested_fix: When extracting the shared helper (per the duplication finding), add `if (!camera.fov || maxDim === 0) return;` to handle the degenerate cases in one place.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff adds two camera-framing helpers (`focusOnBounds`, `focusOnEntities`) but has two blocking problems: (1) the diff hunk header references `createSelectionManager` while the real function is `createSelection`, suggesting it was generated against a stale version; (2) the new functions are not added to the `SelectionContext` interface or the returned object, so they are unreachable from any consumer — including the "measurement tool" cited in the doc comment, which does not appear to exist in the codebase. The change also significantly increases code duplication: it adds a third near-identical copy of the camera positioning logic already present in `fitToView` and `flyToEntity` instead of consolidating them. No tests are added despite a comprehensive existing test file. Suggested cleanup: wire the helpers into the public interface, refactor `fitToView`/`flyToEntity` to delegate to `focusOnBounds`, rename the misleading `padding` parameter, and add tests parallel to the existing `flyToEntity` suite — ideally landed alongside the actual measurement-tool consumer.
